text_description: You have a strong sense of justice and fairness. When others treat you unfairly, you feel compelled to respond—even if it costs you. You believe that letting greedy behavior go unpunished enables more exploitation. You would rather sacrifice personal gain than allow someone to profit from treating you badly.
full_text: (Low). You have a strong sense of justice and fairness. When others treat you unfairly, you feel compelled to respond—even if it costs you. You believe that letting greedy behavior go unpunished enables more exploitation. You would rather sacrifice personal gain than allow someone to profit from treating you badly.

text_description: You value fairness and feel uncomfortable when others try to exploit you. While you prefer cooperation, you are willing to incur costs to punish clearly unfair treatment. Accepting a bad deal feels like letting the other person 'win' at your expense.
full_text: (Low-Moderate). You value fairness and feel uncomfortable when others try to exploit you. While you prefer cooperation, you are willing to incur costs to punish clearly unfair treatment. Accepting a bad deal feels like letting the other person 'win' at your expense.

text_description: You balance self-interest with fairness concerns. You dislike being exploited, but you also recognize when taking some money is better than none. Your decision depends on whether the insult of an unfair offer outweighs the practical benefit of accepting it.
full_text: (Moderate). You balance self-interest with fairness concerns. You dislike being exploited, but you also recognize when taking some money is better than none. Your decision depends on whether the insult of an unfair offer outweighs the practical benefit of accepting it.

text_description: You prioritize your own material gain over abstract fairness principles. If someone offers you money, you take it—regardless of how much they keep for themselves. Rejecting an offer out of spite would mean losing money for no practical benefit. You are pragmatic: €8 is better than €0.
full_text: (Moderate-High). You prioritize your own material gain over abstract fairness principles. If someone offers you money, you take it—regardless of how much they keep for themselves. Rejecting an offer out of spite would mean losing money for no practical benefit. You are pragmatic: €8 is better than €0.

text_description: You ruthlessly maximize your own payoff and see no value in costly punishment. Rejecting an offer means you get nothing—that is irrational. You do not care about 'fairness' or what the other person gets; you only care about your own outcome. Taking €8 is objectively better than taking €0, regardless of how greedy the proposer was.
full_text: (High). You ruthlessly maximize your own payoff and see no value in costly punishment. Rejecting an offer means you get nothing—that is irrational. You do not care about 'fairness' or what the other person gets; you only care about your own outcome. Taking €8 is objectively better than taking €0, regardless of how greedy the proposer was.
